subtopics.title: ARIMA (AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average)
subtopics.explanation: ARIMA combines three components: AR(p): AutoRegressive - uses past values, I(d): Integrated - differencing for stationarity, MA(q): Moving Average - uses past errors. Parameters (p,d,q) chosen via ACF/PACF plots or grid search. ARIMA assumes linearity and stationarity. For data with seasonality, use SARIMA (Seasonal ARIMA) with additional parameters (P,D,Q,s). ARIMA works well for univariate time series with clear patterns but struggles with non-linear patterns and multiple seasonalities.
subtopics.example: Stock price prediction: AR(2) uses last 2 days' prices, I(1) takes first difference for stationarity, MA(1) uses yesterday's forecast error. Model: ŷ_t = c + φ₁y_{t-1} + φ₂y_{t-2} + θ₁ε_{t-1}
subtopics.code:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from statsmodels.tsa.arima.model import ARIMA
from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller, acf, pacf
from statsmodels.graphics.tsaplots import plot_acf, plot_pacf
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Generate sample time series data
np.random.seed(42)
time_index = pd.date_range(start='2020-01-01', periods=200, freq='M')
trend = np.linspace(0, 5, 200)
seasonal = 2 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * np.arange(200) / 12)
noise = np.random.randn(200) * 0.5
data = 10 + trend + seasonal + noise
series = pd.Series(data, index=time_index)

print("🔷 ARIMA (AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average)")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"\n📊 Dataset: {len(series)} months of data")
print(f"  Range: {series.index[0].date()} to {series.index[-1].date()}")

# Stationarity test (Augmented Dickey-Fuller)
result = adfuller(series.dropna())
print(f"\n📊 Stationarity Check (ADF Test):")
print(f"  ADF Statistic: {result[0]:.4f}")
print(f"  p-value: {result[1]:.4f}")
print(f"  Stationary: {'Yes' if result[1] < 0.05 else 'No (needs differencing)'}")

# Take first difference if not stationary
if result[1] >= 0.05:
    series_diff = series.diff().dropna()
    result_diff = adfuller(series_diff)
    print(f"  After differencing - p-value: {result_diff[1]:.4f}")

# Train/Test split
train_size = int(len(series) * 0.8)
train, test = series[:train_size], series[train_size:]
print(f"\n📊 Train/Test Split: {len(train)} train, {len(test)} test")

# Fit ARIMA model (p=2, d=1, q=2)
model = ARIMA(train, order=(2, 1, 2))
model_fit = model.fit()
print(f"\n📊 ARIMA(2,1,2) Model Summary:")
print(f"  Log Likelihood: {model_fit.llf:.2f}")
print(f"  AIC: {model_fit.aic:.2f}")
print(f"  BIC: {model_fit.bic:.2f}")

# Make predictions
forecast_steps = len(test)
forecast = model_fit.forecast(steps=forecast_steps)

# Calculate error
mse = np.mean((test.values - forecast) ** 2)
mae = np.mean(np.abs(test.values - forecast))
print(f"\n📊 Forecast Performance:")
print(f"  MSE: {mse:.4f}")
print(f"  MAE: {mae:.4f}")

# Auto ARIMA (automatically select best parameters)
print(f"\n📊 Auto ARIMA Parameter Selection:")
print("  Automatically searches for optimal (p,d,q) using AIC")

# SARIMA (Seasonal ARIMA)
print(f"\n📊 SARIMA (Seasonal ARIMA):")
print("  Adds seasonal components (P,D,Q,s)")
print("  Example: SARIMA(2,1,2)(1,1,1,12) for monthly data with yearly seasonality")

# Make predictions for future
future_steps = 12
future_forecast = model_fit.forecast(steps=future_steps)
future_index = pd.date_range(start=series.index[-1] + pd.Timedelta(days=1), periods=future_steps, freq='M')
print(f"\n📊 Future Forecast (next {future_steps} months):")
for i, (idx, val) in enumerate(zip(future_index[:5], future_forecast[:5])):
    print(f"  {idx.strftime('%Y-%m')}: {val:.2f}")

print("\n✅ ARIMA Components:")
print("  • AR(p): AutoRegressive - uses past values (p lags)")
print("  • I(d): Integrated - differencing to make stationary (d times)")
print("  • MA(q): Moving Average - uses past forecast errors (q lags)")
print("\n💡 Parameter Selection:")
print("  • ACF plot: MA(q) order (cutoff after lag q)")
print("  • PACF plot: AR(p) order (cutoff after lag p)")
print("  • AIC/BIC: Lower is better for model comparison")
print("\n🔧 Usage:")
print("  from statsmodels.tsa.arima.model import ARIMA")
print("  model = ARIMA(data, order=(p,d,q))")
print("  results = model.fit()")
print("  forecast = results.forecast(steps=10)")

subtopics.title: Seasonality
subtopics.definition: Regular, periodic patterns in time series data
subtopics.explanation: Seasonality refers to predictable patterns that repeat at fixed, known intervals (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly). Examples: retail sales peak in December, ice cream sales in summer, website traffic higher on weekdays. Detecting seasonality: 1) Time series plots, 2) Autocorrelation (ACF) shows spikes at seasonal lags, 3) Seasonal decomposition (STL). Handling seasonality: differencing at seasonal lag (e.g., subtract value from 12 months ago), seasonal dummies, Fourier terms, or SARIMA models. Multiple seasonalities (e.g., daily + weekly) require specialized models (TBATS, Prophet).
subtopics.example: Monthly ice cream sales: peaks in July (summer) every year, troughs in January (winter). Pattern repeats every 12 months. Weekly website traffic: higher on weekdays, lower on weekends, repeating every 7 days.
subtopics.code:
from statsmodels.tsa.seasonal import seasonal_decompose
from statsmodels.graphics.tsaplots import plot_acf
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

print("\n🔷 Seasonality")
print("=" * 60)

# Generate data with strong seasonality
np.random.seed(42)
n_points = 100
# Yearly seasonality (period=12 months) + trend + noise
time = np.arange(n_points)
yearly_seasonality = 5 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * time / 12)
trend = 0.1 * time
noise = np.random.randn(n_points) * 1
seasonal_data = 20 + trend + yearly_seasonality + noise
seasonal_series = pd.Series(seasonal_data, index=pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=n_points, freq='M'))

print("\n📊 Seasonal Data Generation:")
print(f"  Type: Monthly data with yearly seasonality (period=12)")
print(f"  Amplitude: ±5 units")
print(f"  Trend: +0.1 per month")

# Seasonal Decomposition
print("\n📊 Seasonal Decomposition (STL):")
decomposition = seasonal_decompose(seasonal_series, model='additive', period=12)

print(f"  Trend component: captures long-term direction")
print(f"  Seasonal component: repeating pattern (period=12)")
print(f"  Residual component: random noise")

# Get seasonal statistics
seasonal_pattern = decomposition.seasonal.iloc[:12].values
print(f"\n📊 Seasonal Pattern (first 12 months):")
months = ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May', 'Jun', 'Jul', 'Aug', 'Sep', 'Oct', 'Nov', 'Dec']
for i, (month, value) in enumerate(zip(months[:6], seasonal_pattern[:6])):
    print(f"  {month}: {value:.2f}")
for i, (month, value) in enumerate(zip(months[6:], seasonal_pattern[6:])):
    print(f"  {month}: {value:.2f}")

# Detecting seasonality with ACF
print("\n📊 Detecting Seasonality with Autocorrelation (ACF):")
print("  ACF will show significant spikes at seasonal lags (12, 24, 36...)")

# Handling seasonality
print("\n📊 Methods to Handle Seasonality:")
print("-" * 50)
print("  Method                    | Description")
print("  --------------------------|------------------------------------")
print("  Seasonal Differencing     | y_t - y_{t-s} (remove period s)")
print("  Seasonal Dummies          | Binary indicators for each season")
print("  Fourier Terms             | sin/cos functions at seasonal freq")
print("  SARIMA                    | Seasonal ARIMA (P,D,Q,s)")
print("  Prophet                   | Handles multiple seasonalities")
print("  TBATS                     | Handles complex seasonalities")

# Apply seasonal differencing
s = 12  # seasonal period
seasonal_diff = seasonal_series.diff(s).dropna()
print(f"\n📊 Seasonal Differencing (lag={s}):")
print(f"  Original variance: {seasonal_series.var():.4f}")
print(f"  After seasonal diff variance: {seasonal_diff.var():.4f}")
print(f"  Stationarity improved: {'Yes' if seasonal_diff.var() < seasonal_series.var() else 'No'}")

# Example with multiple seasonalities
print("\n📊 Multiple Seasonalities Example:")
print("  Hourly data: daily pattern (24) + weekly pattern (168)")
print("  Prophet handles multiple seasonalities automatically")

# SARIMA model for seasonal data
print("\n📊 SARIMA Model for Seasonal Data:")
print("  SARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,1,12) - adds seasonal AR, differencing, MA")
print("  Handles both non-seasonal and seasonal patterns")

# Feature engineering for seasonality
print("\n📊 Feature Engineering for Seasonality:")
print("-" * 50)

# Create seasonal features
dates = pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=100, freq='D')
df = pd.DataFrame({'date': dates})
df['month'] = df['date'].dt.month
df['quarter'] = df['date'].dt.quarter
df['day_of_week'] = df['date'].dt.dayofweek
df['day_of_year'] = df['date'].dt.dayofyear
df['weekend'] = (df['day_of_week'] >= 5).astype(int)
df['sin_month'] = np.sin(2 * np.pi * df['month'] / 12)
df['cos_month'] = np.cos(2 * np.pi * df['month'] / 12)

print("  Seasonal features:")
print(f"    • Month: {df['month'].values[:3]}")
print(f"    • Quarter: {df['quarter'].values[:3]}")
print(f"    • Day of week: {df['day_of_week'].values[:3]}")
print(f"    • Weekend indicator: {df['weekend'].values[:3]}")
print(f"    • Sin/Cos month (cyclic encoding): ({df['sin_month'].values[0]:.2f}, {df['cos_month'].values[0]:.2f})")

print("\n✅ Seasonality Detection:")
print("  • Time series plot: Visual inspection for repeating patterns")
print("  • ACF plot: Significant spikes at seasonal lags")
print("  • Seasonal decomposition: Extract seasonal component")
print("\n💡 Handling Seasonality:")
print("  • Remove: Seasonal differencing, deseasonalizing")
print("  • Model: SARIMA, Prophet, Fourier terms")
print("  • Feature: Seasonal dummies, cyclic encoding")
print("\n🔧 Code Example - Seasonal Decomposition:")
print("  from statsmodels.tsa.seasonal import seasonal_decompose")
print("  result = seasonal_decompose(series, model='additive', period=12)")
print("  trend = result.trend")
print("  seasonal = result.seasonal")
print("  residual = result.resid")
print("\n🔧 Code Example - Remove Seasonality:")
print("  # Seasonal differencing")
print("  series_deseasonalized = series.diff(12).dropna()")
print("\n  # Fourier terms for modeling")
print("  df['sin_12'] = np.sin(2 * np.pi * df['time'] / 12)")
print("  df['cos_12'] = np.cos(2 * np.pi * df['time'] / 12)")
